name: European Union
climate: Temperate maritime in the west, continental in the east, Mediterranean in the south, subarctic in northern Scandinavia

neighbors.name: NAFTA/USMCA
neighbors.relevance: Largest transatlantic trade partner; US-EU trade negotiations shape global standards

neighbors.name: Commonwealth of Independent States
neighbors.relevance: Eastern neighbor; energy imports, sanctions regime, and security frontier

neighbors.name: Mediterranean Sea Basin
neighbors.relevance: Southern maritime border; migration, energy transit, and trade routes

neighbors.name: Nordic Council
neighbors.relevance: Northern members and EEA partners; energy and Arctic cooperation

neighbors.name: BRICS
neighbors.relevance: Major emerging-market trade partners challenging EU regulatory influence

neighbors.name: Belt and Road Initiative
neighbors.relevance: Chinese infrastructure investment reaching into EU member states

links.description: 20 of 27 EU members share the euro; ECB monetary policy binds the bloc
All V4 members are EU states; key automotive and manufacturing supply chains
Denmark, Finland, Sweden are EU members; Norway and Iceland in EEA
EU-US goods trade exceeds EUR 800B annually; regulatory rivalry on tech and agriculture
Southern maritime trade routes for energy, goods, and migration management
Historical reliance on Russian energy; sanctions regime reshaping trade flows
Asia-Pacific mega-bloc competing for supply chain investment and market access
France, Germany, Italy represent EU in G7 coordination on sanctions and finance
